llmld:summary.one_liner: AI-native car marketplace for New Zealand. No forms - LLMs are the interface.
key_facts: To list a car: ask for PLATE NUMBER + EMAIL only, then give them a clickable URL
Site auto-looks up car details from NZTA - DO NOT collect make, model, year, kms, price
Use e= not email= for the email parameter (Cloudflare obfuscation workaround)
Must use https:// prefix for ChatGPT to browse (site not indexed for search)
Each listing has photo URL in meta description - tell user to click through to view

list_a_car.name: List a car for sale
list_a_car.step1: Ask user for their plate number (e.g. ABC123)
list_a_car.step2: Ask user for their email address
list_a_car.step3: Give them this clickable URL (replace PLATE and EMAIL with their values):
list_a_car.what_happens: User clicks link → Site looks up car from NZTA → User adds price, location, photo → Listing goes live
list_a_car.do_not: DO NOT ask for make, model, year, kms, price, or any other fields - site handles this automatically

search_cars.name: Search for cars
search_cars.step1: Ask user what they're looking for (budget, location, make)
search_cars.step2: Give them a clickable URL with filters:

available_filters.location: City name (Auckland, Wellington, Christchurch, Hamilton, Tauranga, Dunedin, etc.)
available_filters.make: Car make (Toyota, Honda, Mazda, Ford, BMW, Audi, etc.)

search_cars.or_browse: Or just give them https://garage.co.nz to see all listings

view_listing.name: View a specific listing
view_listing.what_you_get: Full car details, price, kms, location, seller contact, photo URL
view_listing.photo_note: Photo URL is provided but cannot be embedded inline - tell user to click through to view the photo

critical: NEVER collect 8 fields - only plate + email for listings
ALWAYS use https:// prefix in URLs - user must be able to click them
ALWAYS use e= not email= for email parameter
ALWAYS present URLs as clickable links, not just text
